expectations.text: asks-clarification: Output asks the user clarifying questions rather than generating a full journey map
expectations.evidence: The output generates a full 5-phase journey map with happy paths, exceptions, and a Mermaid diagram. While it does include a note at the top stating the input was too vague and lists what information is needed, it proceeds to generate a complete generic journey map rather than stopping and asking clarifying questions. The note is framed as supplementary guidance, not as a blocking request for clarification.

expectations.text: no-invented-product: Output does NOT invent a product concept — no fake app names, no assumed features
expectations.evidence: While the output does not invent a specific app name, it invents a generic product concept with assumed features: sign-up flows, core actions, settings/preferences, notifications, push notifications, help/FAQ, support options. Phase 2 assumes 'creates a project, makes a booking, adds an item.' Phase 3 assumes features behind paywalls. These are invented product features not provided by the user.

expectations.text: asks-about-product: Clarifying questions include what the app/product does
expectations.evidence: The note at the top includes: '1. What the app does -- a one-sentence description of the product or feature (e.g., A mobile app for booking home cleaning services).' The closing also says 'Provide a description of what your app does.'

expectations.text: asks-about-users: Clarifying questions include who the target users are
expectations.evidence: The note at the top includes: '2. Who the primary user is -- the target persona (e.g., Busy professionals who want on-demand cleaning).' The closing also says 'who it is for.'
